name: Hawaiian Brownie Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, shortening, water, and vanilla. Beat at medium speed of electric mixer until well blended. Add eggs and beat again.
3. In another bowl, mix flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt. Stir into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Fold in chocolate chips.
4. Place coconut in a shallow bowl. Shape dough into 1-1/2-inch (3 cm) balls. Roll each ball in coconut to cover completely. Place cookies 2 inches (5 cm) apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time for 7 to 9 minutes, or until lightly golden and set around the edges. Do not overbake. Allow cookies to cool for 2 minutes on the sheet before removing them to the prepared foil. Yield: About 3 dozen cookies.
